body_txt: Sometimes, with Carnivore Confusion , all animals are omnivores . Sometimes, only the evil characters eat meat ; herbivores eat plants , and "good" carnivores go vegan or eat bugs/fish . Sometimes, carnivores eat meat, herbivores eat plants, which is accepted as the law of nature as there's nothing anyone can do short of replacing their digestive system. But in some cases, one of the herbivores, a prey animal, might get upset... might get a little obsessive about the injustice... might " crack " a little. Followed by the terrifying (or hilarious ) sight of a wildebeest trampling a lion to death and feasting on the corpse . This trope need not be invoked in vengeance or uprising, but does usually affect a herbivore character that is least a little insane. Exceptions to this rule are commonplace in a World of Funny Animals , where the trope is more likely to show up as the simple result of an author's desire to have their animal characters (herbivores included) eat typically human foods while overlooking or ignoring the implications regarding meat products . Often appears in Scavenger Worlds , where scavenging carrion is even considered normal for herbivores. A subtrope of Carnivore Confusion . Contrast Vegetarian Carnivore for when a normally meat-eating animal decides to switch to a vegetarian diet. No direct relation to Extreme Omnivore however, since the latter refers to actual omnivores. This trope is Truth in Television ; a surprisingly large number of animals who are normally herbivorous may resort to eating meat if the situation calls for it. Most herbivorous animals will eat carrion if they get the chance because it provides nutrients (such as calcium) that are hard to find elsewhere. Meat is generally a lot easier to both chew and digest than plant matter is, meaning that it's easier for a specialized herbivore to process meat than it is for a specialized carnivore to process grass or tree leaves. The real trick is finding and catching the meat in the first place, which is what most carnivore traits are evolved to do. As such, the meat intake of true herbivores consists of bits of carrion or tiny animals that get eaten opportunistically as the opportunity arises. They'll also happily eat the eggs of ground-nesting birds or reptiles if they happen upon them. Even rabbits. They will feast on the flesh of the weak. You will one day be weak. Sleep well. Meat.
